title: Roster CSV/XLSX ingestion: upload → validate → review (valid/invalid/duplicate) → commit, both portals
business_reason: The school-side roster upload only creates an empty batch shell; the core purpose of the module — parse the uploaded student file, validate each row against the rules, write students rows, surface a review of valid/invalid/duplicate rows, and gate lock on zero invalid/blocking-duplicates — is unbuilt (SPEC_VS_BUILT_GAP §3.4/§3.7). Without it the upstream→downstream chain relies on manual status flips and the roster can be locked with unvalidated data.

decisions_locked_by_founder_2026_06_25.data_track: Reuse student_roster_batches; tombstone student_uploads as the orphan track (§3.7).
decisions_locked_by_founder_2026_06_25.file_ingest: CSV + XLSX parsed inline (xlsx dep already present); defer binary file storage + signed-URL retention to a follow-up CR.
decisions_locked_by_founder_2026_06_25.portals: Both school (school_coordinator, source_type=school_uploaded) and staff upload-on-behalf (ops roles, reason-required, source_type=staff_uploaded_on_behalf) in this CR, sharing one ingestion engine.

design.engine: server/lib/rosterIngest.ts (framework, pure): detect format (csv|xlsx), reject forbidden columns (aadhaar/passport/government_id/bank_account), validate each row (student_name trim+normalize+non-blank, grade in olympiad-eligible set, consent_obtained coerced), dedupe within school+participation (school_roll_number else normalized_name+grade), return { validRows, invalidRows[{row,errors}], duplicateRows, counts, validation_report, duplicate_report }.
design.service: server/roster/ingestService.ts (framework): load batch (scope check), run engine, bulk-insert students (valid→status=valid, invalid→status=invalid+validation_errors; school_id from actor, participation_id from batch, candidate_id null until lock), update batch counts/validation_report/duplicate_report and batch_status (validated|validation_failed), audit, idempotent.
design.routes: POST /api/school/roster/[id]/ingest and POST /api/staff/<roster>/[id]/ingest (staff path also accepts source_type=staff_uploaded_on_behalf + reason).
design.review_ui: ModuleTable detail/review surface: valid/invalid/duplicate tabs with per-row validation_errors; PII masked for unprivileged staff; lock action gated (invalid_rows=0 AND blocking duplicates=0).
design.lock_gate: validation_policy.json: invalid_rows_block_lock + blocking_duplicates_block_lock enforced as fail-closed preconditions on submit_for_lock/lock.

param_14.question: How does an uploaded student roster file (CSV/XLSX) get parsed, validated per-row, reviewed, and committed before lock — for both school self-upload and staff upload-on-behalf?
param_14.answer: Reusable ingestion engine parses CSV/XLSX inline, validates each row (required cols, name, grade-in-set, consent, forbidden-col rejection), dedupes within school+participation, writes students rows (valid/invalid), rolls counts + validation/duplicate reports onto the existing student_roster_batches record, surfaces a valid/invalid/duplicate review, and fail-closed-gates lock on zero invalid + zero blocking duplicates. Both portals share the engine; staff path records reason + source_type.
param_14.security_implications: PII (student_name, parent_*, roll) is sensitive — masked for unprivileged staff (P1.9/P2.10); school_id server-derived, browser school_id ignored (P3.8); forbidden government-id columns rejected (mass-assignment / validation_policy forbidden_mvp_fields); validation_errors/report are restricted (audit-only); cross-school IDOR fail-closed via kernel ownsRecord/recordInScope.
param_14.approval_implications: Lock is fail-closed on invalid_rows=0 AND blocking_duplicates=0; reversal (count_finalised→validation_passed) is ops-only, reason-required, blocked if payment_paid/exam_slot_confirmed/materials_released.
param_14.downstream_dependencies: Locked roster feeds candidate_id assignment (CHAIN-003, already built) and fee/finance + exam-slot gates; students.status feeds evaluation/results; duplicate/invalid counts feed reports_exports. validate_upload runs inline (system actor) — not an async job in this CR.
param_14.assumptions: xlsx dep present; inline parse acceptable (≤10MB); binary source-file storage + signed-URL retention deferred (staff_upload_policy source_file_private/retention noted as follow-up); olympiad-eligible grade set sourced from participation/olympiad config or a sane default if absent.
